title: Atmospheric Intelligence Engine (AIE): reference implementation for multi-horizon urban PM2.5 forecasting at Seberang Jaya, Malaysia
description:
Reference implementation accompanying the manuscript Multi-horizon forecasting of urban PM2.5 with a dilated temporal-convolution and transformer hybrid: skill, calibrated uncertainty, and driver sensitivity at a tropical Malaysian site.

Includes an end-to-end pipeline for hourly multi-horizon forecasting (1 h to 168 h), the Atmospheric Intelligence Engine neural architecture with MC-dropout uncertainty quantification, strong baselines (persistence, XGBoost, LSTM), an exploratory driver-sensitivity scenario module, and the plotting scripts that regenerate the manuscript figures from the pipeline artefacts.

The raw observational dataset is governed by a data-use agreement with the Department of Environment Malaysia and is not bundled with this archive; a small deterministically-generated sample dataset with the same schema is included for continuous integration and smoke-testing only, and is explicitly marked as not suitable for scientific inference.
